title: Vafþrúðnismál
stanzas: Odin the All-Father set forth one day,
To test his knowledge against the wise,
To find the giant Vaftrudnir old,
Who was famed for his wisdom great and vast.
When Odin came to the giant's hall,
He spoke thus: 'I have come to thee,
To match my wits against thine own,
And stake my head upon the test.'
Then Vaftrudnir smiled with grim delight,
And said: 'If thy knowledge be not deep,
Thy head shall be the price of this game,
But if thou art wise, thy life is safe.'
Then Odin spoke: 'From what did the earth arise?'
And Vaftrudnir answered with words of power,
'From the body of Ymir was the earth made,
His blood became the sea and stream.'
'From his flesh came the fertile soil,
His bones became the mountains high,
His teeth were scattered as rocks and stones,
And from his skull came the sky above.'
Then Odin asked: 'What holdeth the sun,
And keepeth her in her constant course?'
'Two swift wolves follow her constant trail,
And one shall seize her at the last.'
'What is the name of the steed that beareth,
The sun and moon around the sky?'
'Skinfaxi is the name of that horse so swift,
Whose mane shines bright as the sun itself.'
Then Odin grew weary of questions asked,
And spoke thus to the giant wise,
'Now tell me what thou dost know of the future,
What shall befall when Ragnarok comes?'
Then Vaftrudnir spoke with solemn tone,
'The sun shall fade and the stars grow dark,
The earth shall sink in the sea of death,
And all the mountains shall tumble down.'
'Yet from the sea shall the earth arise,
Green and fertile as before,
And the sun shall have a daughter fair,
To ride the sky when the old one falls.'
When Vaftrudnir saw that Odin knew,
All of the secrets of the world,
He bowed before him with respect,
'Thou art the All-Father, great and wise.'
